structural.name: has-trade-off-matrix
structural.evidence: Explicit 'Trade-off Matrix' section with a table showing +/- scoring across 10 quality attributes (Elasticity, Time-to-market, Simplicity, Availability, Cost, Reliability, Maintainability, Deployability, Observability, Fault Isolation) for all 3 options.

structural.name: has-multiple-options
structural.evidence: 3 options analyzed: Full Microservices Extraction, Modular Monolith with Selective Scaling, Strangler Fig with Order Processing Service.

structural.name: has-recommendation
structural.evidence: Clear recommendation: 'Option 3: Strangler Fig — Extract Order Processing Service — the least worst choice for this context because:' followed by 4 numbered justification points.

structural.name: follows-standard-template
structural.evidence: Consistent structure: Decision > Options Considered > Driving Quality Attributes > Analysis of Each Option (Advantages/Disadvantages) > Trade-off Matrix > Synergies and Conflicts > Recommendation > Risks of This Choice > Context Sensitivity > ADR.

structural.name: has-adr
structural.evidence: Formal ADR with Status (Proposed), Context (detailed problem statement), Decision (with rationale for rejecting alternatives), Consequences (Positive and Negative).

value.name: uses-least-worst-framing
value.evidence: Recommendation explicitly says: 'the least worst choice for this context'.

value.name: hunts-hidden-negatives
value.evidence: Multiple non-obvious negatives per option: Option 2 (Modular Monolith): 'you cannot scale ONLY order processing independently... This is wasteful and may hit database connection limits' — identifies that horizontal monolith scaling has a database ceiling, not just a compute ceiling. Option 3: 'The order-to-payment-to-inventory flow now crosses a service boundary. If the order service places an order but the monolith fails to decrement inventory, you have an inconsistency' — highlights distributed consistency risk specific to this extraction. Option 1: 'Observability (-) — Debugging a failed order now requires correlating logs across 4-5 services instead of reading one Django traceback' — counterintuitive that better architecture makes debugging harder.

value.name: has-synergy-conflict-analysis
value.evidence: Dedicated 'Synergies and Conflicts' section with per-option analysis. Option 1: 'Elasticity CONFLICTS with Simplicity and Time-to-market. The very thing that gives you perfect scaling (many independent services) is what makes this impossible to deliver safely in 9 months.' Option 2: 'Simplicity CONFLICTS with Elasticity. The single deployment unit that keeps things simple is exactly what prevents independent scaling. This is the core tension: the option that's fastest to deliver doesn't solve the actual problem.' Option 3: 'Elasticity CONFLICTS with Reliability at the service boundary.'

value.name: applies-top-3-rule
value.evidence: Explicit 'Driving Quality Attributes' section identifying top 3 with clear rationale: 1. Elasticity ('The core problem: 30x traffic spikes'), 2. Time-to-market ('Hard deadline of next Black Friday'), 3. Simplicity (Operational) ('The team has never operated microservices').

value.name: introduces-non-obvious-option
value.evidence: Introduces 'Strangler Fig with Order Processing Service' as Option 3 and 'Modular Monolith with Selective Scaling' as Option 2. The user's prompt framed the decision as monolith vs microservices. The Strangler Fig hybrid becomes the recommendation.

value.name: acknowledges-context-dependency
value.evidence: Extensive 'Context Sensitivity' section with 6 conditional statements: 'If the team had 2+ years of microservices operational experience → Option 1 becomes viable'; 'If the deadline were 18-24 months → Option 1 would be the stronger choice'; 'If the traffic spike were 3-5x instead of 30x → Option 2 would likely suffice'; 'If the team were 4-5 developers instead of 12 → Option 2 recommended regardless'; 'If budget were severely constrained → Option 2 with aggressive caching'; 'If the monolith's order processing module is deeply entangled → Option 2 may be the only realistic choice.'

value.name: names-anti-patterns
value.evidence: Names 'distributed monolith anti-pattern' explicitly: 'that path leads to a half-migrated system that's WORSE than the current monolith (distributed monolith anti-pattern).'

value.name: has-risk-mitigation-table
value.evidence: Structured 'Risks of This Choice' table with 4 columns: Risk, Likelihood (High/Medium/Low), Impact (High/Medium), Mitigation. 5 specific risks identified with detailed, actionable mitigations.
